summary: Phase 1 MT5 supervision backend regressions + new MT5-mode activation verified. 32/32 backend pytest cases pass (17 from iter_1 still green + 15 new in test_mt5_phase1.py). Mock-mode regression (4): /api/kpis still returns 8 accounts with no 'mt5' source, /api/accounts returns 8 items starting ACC-001, equity/drawdown/trades endpoints serve as before, kill-switch toggles LIVE<->PAUSED. Adapter unit tests (3): drawdown_from_equity([100,120,90]) -> max_dd=-25%, current_dd=-25%, empty list -> ([],0,0); account_from_bridge maps id=MT5-<login>, status logic for connected/trade_allowed/kill_switch, daily_pnl anchor subtraction, source='mt5'. mt5_client.configured_bridges (3): empty env -> [], single MT5_BRIDGE_URL -> 1 entry, MT5_BRIDGE_URLS=a,b,c -> 3 entries with tokens. MT5-mode activation (5): spawned a uvicorn subprocess on an ephemeral port with MT5_BRIDGE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:9999. /api/kpis -> source='mt5', accounts_total=0; /api/accounts -> []; /api/bridge/health -> {configured:1, bridges:[{url,status:'unreachable',error:...}]}; POST /api/accounts/MT5-5609382/kill-switch persists kill_switch=true & PAUSED to Mongo (even with bridge down) and GET account returns 503 because no cached snapshot yet; PUT risk-limits persists single field and merges with defaults across multiple patches. Frontend smoke regression: dashboard loads cleanly, 8 mock accounts render, total equity reads $1,874,080.90 (~$1.87M), KPI ticker + RiskPanel + EquityCurve + Drawdown + Alerts panel all visually identical to iter_1. /app/mt5-bridge/ Windows project structure verified to exist (bridge_server.py, mt5_service.py, storage.py, equity_reconstructor.py, snapshot_recorder.py, run.bat, config.py, requirements.txt) — not exercised on Linux as MetaTrader5 lib is Windows-only, per request.

minor.issue: When bridge is unreachable and no cache exists, GET account returns 503. Considered expected per route logic; UI must handle 503 gracefully (no kill-switch optimistic update visible in account detail). Confirm UX intent.
active_alerts/critical_alerts hard-coded to 0 with comment 'Phase 2' — fine for Phase 1 but the frontend KPI ticker will show '0 active' when in MT5 mode. Acceptable per phasing.

critical_code_review_comments: routes_mt5.py::_resolve_login_to_client iterates clients() and calls .account() on each on every request — N HTTP round-trips per lookup. For multi-bridge deployments this is O(N) per equity/drawdown/trades/positions/orders call. Add a small in-memory or Mongo-backed login->bridge_url mapping (e.g., cache.put('login_map:<login>', url) on first successful resolve).

routes_mt5.py::list_accounts — when the first client fails, it falls back to ALL cached account: docs (limit 50) and `break`s out of the loop even though later configured bridges may still be reachable. The 'break' skips trying bridge #2..N. Loop should `continue` instead of `break` and fall back to cache only after all bridges failed.

mt5_cache.maybe_set_daily_anchor uses date.today() (local TZ) but the rest of the system is UTC-based. Use datetime.now(timezone.utc).date() to avoid a TZ-shift bug at UTC midnight on hosts in non-UTC zones.

mt5_client._list_env reads env at import time only if you call configured_bridges() at import; calling at request time is correct (it currently does). However server.py snapshots MT5_MODE at import — if MT5_BRIDGE_URL is set at runtime via secrets reload, the router never switches. Acceptable for K8s reboots but document.

routes_mt5.py kill-switch endpoint stores 'PAUSED' in cache but if cached payload's prior status was 'ERROR' it keeps 'ERROR' on disable instead of recomputing. After disable, status falls back to cached.payload.get('status') which can be stale.

mt5_adapter.trades_from_deals: 'side' is read from entry['side'] but MT5 deals expose direction as integer DEAL_TYPE_BUY/DEAL_TYPE_SELL — the bridge must convert to BUY/SELL strings before adapter sees them (verify in bridge_server.py).
server.py: CORS middleware is added AFTER include_router — works because routes are matched per-request, but conventionally middleware is added before routers. Cosmetic.
server.py module-level ACCOUNTS/ALERTS state reset on reload (carried from iter_1). Already noted; not in Phase 1 scope.
seed_data_creation: MT5-mode tests created Mongo overrides docs in 'mt5_overrides' collection for login 5609382 (kill_switch and risk_limits). These are harmless and only used when MT5 mode is active.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests in /app/backend/tests/ now contain TWO suites: test_mt5_api.py (iter_1 mock-mode against deployed preview) and test_mt5_phase1.py (Phase 1: regression + adapter unit + bridge config + MT5-mode subprocess). The MT5-mode tests spawn uvicorn on an ephemeral port with MT5_BRIDGE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:9999 and MT5_BRIDGE_TOKEN=test-token; they DO NOT touch /app/backend/.env so the preview stays in mock mode for the user. If you need to retest, run: `cd /app && python -m pytest backend/tests/ -v`. To manually inspect MT5 mode UX: `cd /app/backend && MT5_BRIDGE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:9999 uvicorn server:app --port 8002` then curl http://127.0.0.1:8002/api/{kpis,accounts,bridge/health}. The mt5-bridge Windows project at /app/mt5-bridge/ was not executed on Linux (MetaTrader5 lib is Windows-only); only its files were verified to exist.
rca of the issue: N/A — no blocking issues. One initial test failure was caused by Motor binding to a per-request asyncio loop when using FastAPI TestClient with module-scope AsyncIOMotorClient created at import time. Resolved by switching the MT5-mode tests to a uvicorn subprocess + requests, which mirrors production execution and gives a single persistent event loop for Motor.
